claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 13 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start by gathering some information about your situation.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions. Child Benefit is assessed for each child individually, so let me start with:

**Do you have a child or children that you're responsible for? If yes, how old is your child (or how old are your children)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. My son Alex is 13 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information. Since Alex is 13 years old, he falls into the under-16 category. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: **Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't work at all. He's only 13, so he's still in school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's good to know. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: **Is Alex receiving any benefits in his own right, such as Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't receive any benefits in his own right.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: **Is Alex in any type of apprenticeship program?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in any apprenticeship. He's just in regular school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect. Now I need to ask you some questions about your own circumstances.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: **Do you normally live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK. I'm currently living abroad.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I see. Since you're living abroad, let me ask some follow-up questions to see if you might still qualify under special circumstances.

**Are you a Crown servant (for example, a diplomat or member of HM Forces) living abroad?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant or anything like that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Let me also check if you have any connection to the UK through the EU Settlement Scheme.

**Do you have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't have settled or pre-settled status.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for answering all my questions. I've now gathered enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

**Assessment Complete**

Based on the information you've provided, I'm afraid you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex.

**Reason:** You do not meet the UK residency requirements for Child Benefit. To be eligible, you must either:
- Normally live in the UK, OR
- Have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme (with certain conditions), OR
- Be a Crown servant living abroad, OR
- Be living in certain qualifying countries

Since you are living abroad and don't fall into any of the special circumstance categories, you do not meet the residency requirements, even though Alex would otherwise qualify as he is under 16, not working, not receiving benefits in his own right, and not in a disqualifying apprenticeship.

If your circumstances change and you move back to the UK or obtain settled/pre-settled status, you may become eligible to claim Child Benefit at that time.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Thank you for explaining that to me. I understand now that because I don't live in the UK and don't meet any of the special circumstances, I'm not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex, even though he would otherwise qualify. I appreciate you taking the time to go through all the questions with me.
